meta.name: best-of-Agent-Harnesses
meta.description: Hand-curated, ranked list of AI agent harnesses, orchestration frameworks, and harness techniques.
meta.tier_help: Adoption surface area, least to most: tier_rank 1 = format-only/single concept, 4 = platform with its own runtime and ecosystem.
meta.autonomy_help: Designed autonomy regime, least to most: rank 1 = human approves each action, 4 = built for unattended runs and fleets. rank 0 / 'n/a' = doesn't own an agent loop.
meta.recovery_help: Behavior when a run dies mid-task, weakest to strongest: rank 1 = start over, 4 = persisted execution state survives restarts. rank 0 / 'n/a' = doesn't execute.

categories.title: Progressive disclosure harnesses
categories.subtitle: Formats, runtimes, and patterns that reveal context, tools, or instructions in layers—index first, details on demand—to control tokens and improve agent focus (the "map, not encyclopedia" principle).

categories.title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)
categories.subtitle: Turnkey coding agents you install and run: IDE extensions, terminal CLIs, Dockerized workspaces. Each entry notes which part is the harness (the agent loop, tool wiring, approval model) versus the UI shell (VS Code extension, TUI, browser client).

categories.title: Coding harness configs and SDKs
categories.subtitle: Skill packs, slash-command libraries, meta-prompting frameworks, and official SDKs that give you the harness (the agent loop, planning, memory, hooks) without bundling a specific IDE or CLI shell.

categories.title: Personal agent runtimes
categories.subtitle: Always-on, self-hosted agents you run as a daemon and talk to from chat apps: gateway runtimes, second brains, and self-improving assistants. The agent as a product you operate, not a library you build with.

categories.title: Frameworks
categories.subtitle: General-purpose agent and LLM application frameworks (the app layer, not harnesses per se).

categories.title: Multi-agent and orchestration
categories.subtitle: Harnesses and patterns for multi-agent coordination and handoffs.

categories.title: Plugins, MCPs, CLI tools
categories.subtitle: IDE plugins, concrete MCP servers, and CLI tools that give agents tools and context.

categories.title: Evaluation and benchmarking harnesses
categories.subtitle: Agentic eval systems, reasoning benchmarks, and open agent benchmarks.

categories.title: Research and task-specific harnesses
categories.subtitle: Deep research, document QA, and domain-specific agent loops.

categories.title: Libraries and SDKs
categories.subtitle: Lightweight runtimes, tool loops, and provider-agnostic harness primitives.

use_cases.intent: I want a turnkey coding agent today
use_cases.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

use_cases.intent: I want an always-on personal agent that lives in my chat apps
I want to extend Claude Code, Codex, or OpenCode with skills and slash commands
I want to build my own coding harness from scratch
I want a drop-in memory layer for agents
I want to plug hundreds to thousands of tools without context bloat
I want a general LLM app framework
I want sandboxed code execution for agent-generated code
I want to evaluate or benchmark agents
I want a deep research / autonomous research agent
I want a provider-agnostic LLM pipe (not a framework)

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want a turnkey coding agent today?
faq.a: Top picks: opencode, Cline, Codex. See the “Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want an always-on personal agent that lives in my chat apps?
faq.a: Top picks: OpenClaw, Hermes, Khoj. See the “Personal agent runtimes” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want to extend Claude Code, Codex, or OpenCode with skills and slash commands?
faq.a: Top picks: Anthropic Skills, everything-claude-code, superpowers. See the “Coding harness configs and SDKs” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want to build my own coding harness from scratch?
faq.a: Top picks: Claude Agent SDK, Google ADK, AutoHarness. See the “Coding harness configs and SDKs” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want a drop-in memory layer for agents?
faq.a: Top picks: Mem0, claude-mem, agentlog. See the “Plugins, MCPs, CLI tools” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want to plug hundreds to thousands of tools without context bloat?
faq.a: Top picks: MCP-Zero, ToolGen, ToolRAG. See the “Progressive disclosure harnesses” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want multi-agent orchestration?
faq.a: Top picks: openai-agents-python, crewAI, autogen. See the “Multi-agent and orchestration” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want a general LLM app framework?
faq.a: Top picks: langgraph, langchain, llama-index. See the “Frameworks” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want low-code / visual workflows?
faq.a: Top picks: langflow, Flowise, Dify. See the “Frameworks” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want browser-using agents?
faq.a: Top picks: browser-use, WebVoyager, puppeteer-real-browser-mcp. See the “Plugins, MCPs, CLI tools” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want sandboxed code execution for agent-generated code?
faq.a: Top picks: E2B, Daytona, smolagents. See the “Libraries and SDKs” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want to evaluate or benchmark agents?
faq.a: Top picks: SWE-bench, AgencyBench, inspect_ai. See the “Evaluation and benchmarking harnesses” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want a deep research / autonomous research agent?
faq.a: Top picks: deepagents, gpt-researcher, openagents. See the “Research and task-specific harnesses” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: What is the best agent harness if I want a provider-agnostic LLM pipe (not a framework)?
faq.a: Top picks: LiteLLM, vercel/ai. See the “Libraries and SDKs” category for the full ranked list.

faq.q: Which agent harnesses can run unattended (headless)?
faq.a: Harnesses designed for unattended runs, batches, and fleets: opencode, OpenHands, goose, SWE-agent, Claude Agent SDK, RepoMaster, OpenClaw, Hermes.

faq.q: Which agent harnesses survive a crash mid-task (durable)?
faq.a: Harnesses whose execution state persists across restarts: langgraph-bigtool, n8n, langgraph, mastra, letta, deepagents, pydantic-ai, Cloudflare Agents.

faq.q: How many of these agent harnesses are open source?
faq.a: 93 of 106 carry a standard open-source license; the rest are source-available or unclear, and flagged per row.

faq.q: What is an agent harness?
faq.a: The runtime that turns a model into an agent: it decides what the model's reasoning is allowed to touch, and supplies the orchestration, tool wiring, memory, error recovery, and guardrails around per-turn inference.

faq.q: How is this list ranked?
faq.a: By relevance to harness concerns (environment, orchestration, lifecycle, guardrails) and by GitHub stars (captured 2026-07-03); each project also carries an adoption-surface tier and autonomy/recovery scores.

faq.q: How can an AI agent use this list directly?
faq.a: Three machine-readable surfaces: harnesses.json (structured), llms.txt (one file), and an MCP server (uvx agent-harnesses-mcp) exposing pick_harness and search_harnesses.

comparisons.title: How to pick a harness
comparisons.summary: Six questions, in order. Each one eliminates most of the list; by the end you should be choosing between two or three projects, not 103. The [use-case index](../README.md#pick-by-use-case), the [landscape charts](../README.md#the-landscape-at-a-glance), and the head-to-head pages do the heavy liftin

comparisons.title: Agent memory layers: Mem0 vs Letta vs claude-mem
comparisons.summary: "Add memory to my agent" hides three different products: a **memory API** you call from any agent (Mem0), an **agent runtime** where memory is the core abstraction (Letta), and a **plugin** that gives one specific harness perfect recall (claude-mem). They're barely competitors — the comparison that

comparisons.title: Multi-agent orchestration: OpenAI Agents SDK vs CrewAI vs AutoGen vs LangGraph
comparisons.summary: Four very different answers to "how should multiple agents coordinate?" — and the differences are architectural, not cosmetic. Picking wrong here is expensive: the coordination model shapes your whole codebase.

comparisons.title: OpenClaw vs Hermes: the always-on personal-agent debate
comparisons.summary: The loudest harness argument of 2026. Both are open-source (MIT), self-hosted, always-on personal agents you talk to from chat apps — and they're two of the most-starred projects on this entire list. The flame war treats them as rivals; the architecture says they're opposite design philosophies that

comparisons.title: Terminal coding agents: opencode vs Codex vs Gemini CLI vs crush vs goose
comparisons.summary: The most-asked pick in this list: *"I want a turnkey coding agent in my terminal today."* These five are the open(ish)-source field. The closed first-party products (Claude Code, Cursor's agent) aren't list entries, but they define the workflow all five implement: a tool-call loop with file edit, sh

projects.name: awesome-cursorrules
projects.description: Curated .cursorrules and skills that leverage Cursor's index-then-load model; the canonical collection for rules-as-progressive-disclosure in the IDE.

projects.name: agents.md
projects.description: Open format for repo-scoped agent briefings; v1.1 adds hierarchical scope and progressive disclosure so agents get a map of what exists, then load only what's relevant.

projects.name: langgraph-bigtool
projects.description: Build LangGraph agents with large tool sets; retrieval and on-demand tool loading so agents scale beyond context without stuffing every schema upfront.

projects.name: MCP-Zero
projects.description: Active tool discovery for autonomous agents: model requests tools by requirement; hierarchical semantic routing over 308 servers / 2,797 tools with ~98% token reduction (APIBank).

projects.name: ToolGen
projects.description: ICLR 2025: unified tool retrieval and calling via generation; 47k+ tools without context stuffing—retrieval and invocation in one generative step.

projects.name: ToolRAG
projects.description: Semantic tool retrieval for LLMs; serves only the tools the user query demands (MCP-compatible), unlimited tool sets with zero context penalty.

projects.name: opencode
projects.description: Open-source terminal coding agent (formerly `sst/opencode`; transferred to anomalyco). The **harness** is a multi-provider tool-call loop (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, local) with strong plugin and MCP support; the TUI is the shell. 100% OSS, very actively shipped.
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

projects.name: Gemini CLI
projects.description: Google's first-party terminal agent for Gemini. The **harness** is the plugin/MCP tool-call loop; the terminal is the shell—Google's parallel to Claude Code / Codex, not just an API.
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

projects.name: Codex
projects.description: OpenAI's terminal coding agent. The **harness** is the sandboxed tool-call loop with multi-provider support; the CLI is the shell. Reference implementation for "official CLI that ships code."
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

projects.name: OpenHands
projects.description: Dockerized software-engineering agent. The **harness** is the bash/editor/browser toolset with micro-agents and event-stream session bridging; Docker is the sandbox. Main OSS choice for teams self-hosting autonomous repo work.
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)
projects.axis: complex (Docker runtime, multi-surface agent — product suite)

projects.name: Open Interpreter
projects.description: Lightweight terminal coding agent oriented to open models (DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen). The **harness** is a code-execution loop — the model writes code, the harness executes it with confirmation gates; the CLI is the shell. The original "let the LLM run code on my machine" project, reborn for open weights.
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

projects.name: Cline
projects.description: VS Code extension whose **harness** is a plan-then-act loop with per-step human approval and cost transparency; the VS Code integration is the UI shell. Open-source counterweight to Cursor.
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

projects.name: goose
projects.description: Block-originated Rust agent, now stewarded by the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation (`aaif-goose/goose`). The **harness** is the MCP/ACP extension model with recipes and provider choice; there's no fixed UI slot—you bolt it into whatever shell you use.
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

projects.name: crush
projects.description: Charm's terminal coding agent (Charm's fork of the original OpenCode). The **harness** is the tool-calling loop with session persistence; the Bubble Tea TUI is the shell.
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

projects.name: claw-code-agent
projects.description: Python reimplementation of the Claude Code agent architecture with zero external dependencies; interactive chat, streaming, plugin runtime, nested agent delegation, cost tracking, MCP transport—portable harness without the Rust/TS toolchain.
projects.category_title: Coding agent products (IDEs, CLIs, full suites)

projects.name: superpowers
projects.description: Performance-oriented harness pack for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor: skills, instincts, memory, security, research-first workflows. Treats harness engineering itself as the performance lever.

projects.name: everything-claude-code
projects.description: The breakout 2026 harness pack for Claude Code: 28 specialized subagents, 119 reusable skills, 60 slash commands, 34 rules, 20+ automated hooks. Ships a full "AI engineering team" as config.

projects.name: Anthropic Skills
projects.description: Anthropic's official Agent Skills repository: SKILL.md-based folders (instructions, scripts, resources) Claude dynamically loads on Claude Code, Claude.ai, and the API. The reference for progressive-disclosure skill packs in 2026.

projects.name: GStack
projects.description: Garry Tan's Claude Code skill stack: 23 slash-command modes (CEO/eng/design review, QA, ship, browse, retro, …) that structure one assistant as a virtual engineering team. Daily driver while running YC.

projects.name: SWE-agent
projects.description: LM-driven harness built for SWE-bench: edit state, command execution, and issue-focused loop—the reference agent stack next to the benchmark itself.

projects.name: Claude Agent SDK
projects.description: Official Anthropic SDK (Python + [TypeScript](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-agent-sdk-typescript), [demos](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-agent-sdk-demos), [quickstarts](https://github.com/anthropics/claude-quickstarts)): built-in tools, MCP, long-running coding agents with session bridging.
projects.axis: complex (full SDK, session bridging — product suite)

projects.name: RepoMaster
projects.description: Repo-scoped research harness: builds function-call and module-dependency graphs to explore only what's needed; large relative gains on MLE-bench and GitTaskBench with lower token use.

projects.name: AutoHarness
projects.description: Lightweight governance harness: wraps any LLM client in ~2 lines for automated harness engineering—6–14 step pipeline, YAML constitution, risk-pattern matching, session persistence with cost tracking, multi-agent profiles.

projects.name: pmstack
projects.description: Claude Code config for AI product managers: CLAUDE.md plus skills for competitive analysis, PRD-from-signal, metric frameworks, stakeholder briefs, and agent eval design. "GStack for PMs."

projects.name: OpenClaw
projects.description: Self-hosted, always-on personal agent (formerly Clawdbot/Moltbot): a gateway + event-loop runtime that treats messages, heartbeats, crons, and webhooks as one input queue, persists state to local files, and lives in your chat apps (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord). 13,700+ community skills; the fastest-growing repo in GitHub history.
projects.axis: complex (always-on runtime, channels, skill ecosystem — product suite)

projects.name: Hermes
projects.description: Nous Research's self-improving agent: a learning loop turns experience into reusable skills, builds a persistent user model across sessions, and checkpoints state to disk with rollback; lean enough for a $5 VPS, driven from chat, and model-agnostic (Nous Portal, OpenRouter, OpenAI, or any endpoint).
projects.axis: slightly complex (lean runtime, learning loop, disk-first memory)

projects.name: Khoj
projects.description: Self-hostable "AI second brain": answers over your docs and the web, custom agents, scheduled automations, and multi-client reach (web, Obsidian, Emacs, WhatsApp). A personal-agent harness with retrieval at the core.

projects.name: Eliza
projects.description: Open "agentic operating system" (elizaOS): persistent multi-agent runtime with character files, a plugin ecosystem, and social/platform integrations — the harness behind a large share of autonomous social agents.

projects.name: Agent Zero
projects.description: Organic, prompt-defined personal agent framework: hierarchical sub-agents, persistent memory, browser and code tools, and self-modifying behavior; runs in Docker with a web UI.

projects.name: OpenHarness (HKUDS)
projects.description: Open agent harness with a built-in personal agent ("Ohmo") that runs across Feishu, Slack, Telegram, and Discord; core tool-use, skills, memory, multi-agent coordination with auto-compaction for multi-day sessions.

projects.name: AIlice
projects.description: Fully autonomous general-purpose agent; one binary, Docker-ready, for when you want "set goal and walk away" without a framework.

projects.name: n8n
projects.description: Fair-code workflow engine with 400+ nodes and native AI nodes; the self-hosted Zapier that actually does agents and LangChain.
projects.axis: complex (400+ nodes, workflow engine — product suite)

projects.name: AutoGPT
projects.description: The original autonomous loop: goal in, agent iterates with tools and memory; Forge is the dev framework, Benchmark the eval harness.
projects.axis: complex (autonomous loop, tools, memory — product suite)

projects.name: langflow
projects.description: Low-code UI to build and deploy LangChain/LangGraph flows; visual DAG editor and one-click run.

projects.name: Dify
projects.description: One-stop LLM app platform: visual workflows, RAG pipeline, 50+ tools, model management; "ship from prototype to prod" in a single UI.

projects.name: langchain
projects.description: Chains, tools, retrievers, and agents; the usual entry point for "add tools to an LLM" in Python/JS.

projects.name: browser-use
projects.description: Python layer over Playwright: natural-language goals become browser actions—web-agent loop without hand-rolling MCP or a custom driver for every site.

projects.name: Flowise
projects.description: Drag-and-drop LangChain UI; deploy flows without code. The low-code sibling to Langflow, with a different component and hosting story.

projects.name: llama-index
projects.description: Data-centric: indexing, RAG, and query engines; agent abstractions sit on top of your data pipelines.

projects.name: agno
projects.description: Python agents with memory, knowledge bases, tools, and structured outputs; continues the PhiData-era product line under the Agno name—production apps, evals, and pipelines.

projects.name: langgraph
projects.description: State-machine graphs over LLM steps; checkpointing, human-in-the-loop, and durable execution so workflows survive restarts.

projects.name: semantic-kernel
projects.description: Microsoft's plugin and planner layer for LLMs; C#, Python, Java; strong on enterprise auth and orchestration.

projects.name: mastra
projects.description: TypeScript-first; agents, tools, and workflows with a single runtime and minimal boilerplate.

projects.name: letta
projects.description: Python agent runtime with tool use and control flow; lean API; stateful agents with long-horizon memory.

projects.name: rasa
projects.description: Conversational AI stack (NLU, dialogue, actions); long-standing OSS choice for chat and voice bots.

projects.name: Google ADK
projects.description: Google's official Agent Development Kit: code-first Python toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying agents. Optimized for Gemini but model-agnostic; deploys to Cloud Run / Vertex AI; ships a dev UI with eval and a code-execution sandbox.
projects.axis: complex (official Google SDK, eval, deploy — product suite)

projects.name: botpress
projects.description: Visual bot builder and runtime; multi-channel, open-source alternative to commercial bot platforms.

projects.name: R2R
projects.description: RAG-first: hybrid search, knowledge graphs, multimodal; the framework for "production RAG" when you care more about retrieval than chat UI.

projects.name: agent-squad
projects.description: AWS-originated orchestrator (now under 2FastLabs): intent classification, streaming, SupervisorAgent; "agent-as-tools" so one agent delegates to a squad.

projects.name: AgentVerse
projects.description: Task-solving and simulation envs for multi-LLM agents; deploy many agents in custom environments without building infra from scratch.

projects.name: Bee Agent Framework
projects.description: Python + TypeScript, LF AI–backed; MCP/ACP, workflows, Requirement Agent; the one that pushes "production multi-agent" without LangChain.

projects.name: AgentStack
projects.description: Scaffolds full agent projects; plugs in CrewAI, LangGraph, OpenAI Swarm, LlamaStack and wires AgentOps observability from day one.

projects.name: AgentSilex
projects.description: ~300 lines of readable agent code on top of LiteLLM; the "I want to see the whole loop" option for learning or minimal production.

projects.name: SuperAgentX
projects.description: Lightweight multi-agent orchestrator with an AGI-angle; minimal surface, docs-first, for teams that want orchestration without the kitchen sink.

projects.name: MetaGPT
projects.description: The "AI software company" multi-agent framework: role-played PM, architect, and engineer agents turn a one-line requirement into specs, designs, and code along an SOP assembly line. The landmark of the genre; development pace has slowed in 2026.

projects.name: autogen
projects.description: Conversable agents and group chats; code execution and human-in-the-loop; Microsoft origin, AG2 ecosystem.
projects.axis: complex (group chat, code exec, AG2 — product suite)

projects.name: crewAI
projects.description: Role-based agents (roles, goals, backstories) in Crews; Flows add event-driven and hierarchical control for production.

projects.name: ChatDev
projects.description: Multi-agent software-company simulation (CEO, CTO, programmer, tester) built on chat chains with communicative dehallucination; ChatDev 2.0 continues the line. MetaGPT's conversational sibling.

projects.name: openai-agents-python
projects.description: Handoffs, guardrails, and multi-LLM routing; minimal surface so you own the loop.

projects.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
projects.description: Microsoft's convergence of AutoGen and Semantic Kernel: build, orchestrate, and deploy agents and multi-agent workflows in Python and .NET, with graph-based workflows and checkpointing — the designated successor harness for both lines.

projects.name: PraisonAI
projects.description: Autonomous multi-agent teams with a single entry point; emphasis on minimal config.

projects.name: AgentRL
projects.description: Multitask, multiturn RL for LLM agents; Ray-based scaling, rollout/actor workers—for teams that want to train agents, not just run them.
projects.axis: complex (RL, Ray, train agents — product suite)

projects.name: claude-mem
projects.description: Claude Code plugin that captures everything an agent does during a session, AI-compresses it (via claude-agent-sdk), and injects the relevant context into future sessions—session-to-session memory as a drop-in.

projects.name: aider
projects.description: Git-aware CLI pair programmer; edits in-repo, supports multiple models and MCP so agents see version control and tools.

projects.name: continue
projects.description: Open-source IDE extension (VS Code, JetBrains); in-editor completion and chat with local or API models.

projects.name: github-mcp-server
projects.description: GitHub's official MCP server (Go): repos, issues, PRs, code search, Actions. Replaces the older community `cyanheads/github-mcp-server` as the canonical way to give agents GitHub access.

projects.name: MCP Python SDK
projects.description: Official SDK to build and consume MCP servers/clients in Python; stdio and SSE transports.

projects.name: MCP TypeScript SDK
projects.description: Official MCP implementation for Node/TS; reference for the protocol.

projects.name: MCP Inspector
projects.description: GUI to test and debug MCP servers; inspect tools, resources, and prompts.

projects.name: MCP Registry
projects.description: Official, community-driven registry for MCP servers—the "app store" MCP clients use to discover servers. Maintained by Anthropic + ecosystem maintainers; v0.1 API frozen, production-grade.

projects.name: Docker MCP Gateway
projects.description: Docker's official MCP CLI plugin / gateway; container-aware MCP tooling from Docker (replaces deprecated `docker/mcp-servers` path).

projects.name: puppeteer-real-browser-mcp
projects.description: Puppeteer MCP with real-browser and anti-detection; for agents that need to drive sites that block headless.

projects.name: Better-OpenCodeMCP
projects.description: MCP server for OpenCode/Crush: async task execution, model bridging (e.g. Claude→Gemini), process pooling.

projects.name: agentlog
projects.description: Persistent decision memory for any project: `remember`, `recall`, `reflect`. Single-file Python CLI that stores decisions as JSONL and uses Claude or Gemini to retrieve and synthesize patterns—Karpathy's LLM Wiki concept as a CLI.

projects.name: Agent Lightning
projects.description: Microsoft's training-oriented harness: optimization loops for agent behavior—when you need to improve policies over rollouts, not only score a fixed prompt.
projects.axis: complex (agent training, Microsoft stack — product suite)

projects.name: SWE-bench
projects.description: LMs resolve real GitHub issues; Docker harness, instance IDs; standard for code-agent evals.

projects.name: AgentBench
projects.description: ICLR'24 benchmark: agents across AlfWorld, DB, knowledge graphs, OS, webshop; Docker Compose, function-calling interface.

projects.name: inspect_ai
projects.description: Inspect AI core: composable eval tasks, sandboxes, scorers, and multi-model runs; the framework behind inspect_evals, not just the task bundle.
projects.axis: complex (eval framework, AISI stack — product suite)

projects.name: WebArena
projects.description: Realistic web env (e.g. e‑commerce, CMS, dev tools); 812 tasks; measures end-to-end web agent success.
projects.axis: complex (812 tasks, web env — product suite)

projects.name: WebVoyager
projects.description: End-to-end web agent with LMMs: screenshots + actions on real sites; benchmark on 15 sites, GPT-4V for automatic eval.

projects.name: ARC-AGI-2
projects.description: ARC Prize task set: grid-based abstraction/reasoning; public and private splits for generalization.

projects.name: SWE-Gym
projects.description: Training and evaluation for SWE agents and verifiers (ICML 2025).

projects.name: swe-smith
projects.description: Data generation for SWE agents; 50k+ instances across 128 repos; used for SWE-agent-LM training.

projects.name: inspect_evals
projects.description: UK AISI/Arcadia/Vector: GAIA and other evals in Inspect AI; level 1–3, sandboxed, tool-calling solvers.

projects.name: arc-agi-benchmarking
projects.description: Runner for ARC-AGI: multi-provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, etc.), rate limits, retries, and scoring.

projects.name: VitaBench
projects.description: ICLR'26: 66 tools, real-world apps (delivery, travel, retail); 100 cross-scenario + 300 single-scenario tasks; adopted by Qwen/Seed.

projects.name: AgencyBench
projects.description: Long-horizon agent benchmark: 32 scenarios, 138 tasks, ~1M tokens and ~90 tool calls; Docker sandbox and rubric-based + LLM judges.
projects.axis: complex (32 scenarios, Docker, judges — product suite)

projects.name: letta-evals
projects.description: Eval harness for stateful Letta agents; configurable suites and grading (LLM or rule-based) so you can measure what you ship.

projects.name: SUPER
projects.description: Agents that set up and run ML/NLP from GitHub repos; 45 expert problems, 152 masked tasks, 602 AutoGen tasks; Docker-based.

projects.name: TRAIL
projects.description: Trace reasoning and agentic issue localization; 148 long-context traces, 841 errors, 20+ error types; Hugging Face dataset.

projects.name: gpt-researcher
projects.description: Autonomous deep-research agent: web + local sources, citation-grounded reports, multi-agent and deep-research modes. The reference open-source research harness.

projects.name: openagents
projects.description: Platform for autonomous agents and autopilot-style workflows; decentralized/Nostr-oriented (Pylon runtime, actively shipped in 2026).

projects.name: Daytona
projects.description: Elastic dev environments for AI-generated code: workspaces, Git, previews—infra harness between "the model wrote a patch" and "it ran in a real machine."

projects.name: Mem0
projects.description: Universal memory layer for AI agents: stores user/org/session memory, retrieves on demand. Apache-2.0; the de-facto memory primitive paired with most harnesses in 2026.

projects.name: LiteLLM
projects.description: One interface to 100+ LLMs; routing, caching, budgets. Not an agent framework—the pipe every agent framework uses.

projects.name: Composio
projects.description: 1,000+ toolkits with auth, tool search, and a sandboxed workbench—drop-in tool layer so agents stop reinventing OAuth + integrations. Python and TypeScript.
projects.axis: complex (1k+ tools, auth, search — product suite)

projects.name: smolagents
projects.description: Code-as-action agents: model outputs Python executed in sandbox (E2B, Modal, etc.); ~1k LOC core.

projects.name: deepagents
projects.description: LangChain's Python+TypeScript agent harness on top of LangGraph: planning tool, virtual filesystem, shell sandbox, sub-agent spawning—the "Claude Code-style" harness as a reusable library.

projects.name: vercel/ai
projects.description: React and Node SDK for streaming, tool calls, and agent-style UIs; provider-agnostic.

projects.name: pydantic-ai
projects.description: Type-safe Python agents with Pydantic I/O; multi-provider, MCP, Logfire observability, and human-in-the-loop.

projects.name: E2B
projects.description: Firecracker sandboxes for executing agent-generated code; the hosted isolation layer many tool-calling demos use instead of running arbitrary LLM output on your laptop.

projects.name: strands-agents
projects.description: Model-driven Python SDK; decorators for tools, native MCP, multi-agent; "minimal code" without sacrificing provider choice.

projects.name: Cloudflare Agents
projects.description: Persistent, stateful agents on Durable Objects: state, websockets, scheduling, and AI chat baked in. The serverless answer to "where does the agent live?"

projects.name: openai-agents-js
projects.description: Official OpenAI Agents SDK for Node/TS: handoffs, guardrails, voice; the JS counterpart to openai-agents-python.

projects.name: open-harness
projects.description: TypeScript Agent class on Vercel AI SDK; streaming events, filesystem/bash tools, MCP, and subagent delegation.

projects.name: Community-curated agent lists
projects.description: Broader directories: e.g. [brandonhimpfen/awesome-ai-agents](https://github.com/brandonhimpfen/awesome-ai-agents), [axioma-ai-labs/awesome-ai-agent-frameworks](https://github.com/axioma-ai-labs/awesome-ai-agent-frameworks), [mb-mal/awesome-ai-agents-frameworks](https://github.com/mb-mal/awesome-ai-agents-frameworks)—differ by scope and update cadence.
